learningObjectives: Use core personal relationship vocabulary at A2 level.
Use present perfect with ever/never to talk about experiences with friends and neighbours.
Use because, when and although in sentences about relationships.
Understand opinions in short online texts.
Listen to short extracts and identify main ideas about invitations and apologies.
Write a short message to apologise or accept an invitation.
Answer interview questions about friendships and neighbours.

vocabularyBank.exampleSentence: Our neighbour often brings us fruit from her garden.
I got an invitation to Mai's birthday party.
He called to apologise for being late.
We congratulated her on her new job.
They have a good relationship with their neighbours.
I trust my best friend with my secrets.
Brothers sometimes argue about small things.
She forgave him after he apologised.
Their friendship started in primary school.
Good friends support each other in difficult times.
He felt jealous when his friend got a new phone.
My mum invited a colleague to dinner.

grammarReference.explanation: Use present perfect (have/has + past participle) with ever or never to talk about life experiences up to now. Ever goes in questions; never replaces not.
examples: I have never argued with my neighbour.
Have you ever congratulated a friend online?
She has always trusted her best friend.
commonMistakes: I never have met her (×) → I have never met her (✓)
Did you ever visit them? (×) → Have you ever visited them? (✓)
She has never forgive him (×) → She has never forgiven him (✓)

grammarReference.explanation: Use because to give a reason, when to show time, and although to show contrast. At A2, keep clauses short and clear.
examples: I apologised because I was late.
When we argue, we usually forgive each other quickly.
Although we are neighbours, we are also close friends.
commonMistakes: I was late because of I missed the bus (×) → I was late because I missed the bus (✓)
Although it was raining, but we went out (×) → Although it was raining, we went out (✓)
When I will arrive, call me (×) → When I arrive, call me (✓)

lessons.title: Lesson 1: Personal Relationship Words
lessons.learningObjective: Recognise and understand twelve personal relationship words at KET A2 level.

exercises.title: Learn: Relationship Word Match
exercises.instructions: Read each sentence. Choose the best word.

questions.questionText: A person who lives next to your house is your _____.
questions.explanation: Neighbour (hàng xóm) là người sống cạnh nhà bạn.

choices.distractorNote: Works with you, not next door
A message to come to an event

questions.questionText: A card or message asking you to come to a party is an _____.
questions.explanation: Invitation (lời mời) mời bạn đến sự kiện.

questions.questionText: When you say sorry for a mistake, you _____.
questions.explanation: Apologise (xin lỗi) khi bạn nhận lỗi.

questions.questionText: When your friend passes an exam, you _____ them.
questions.explanation: Congratulate (chúc mừng) khi ai đó thành công.

questions.questionText: The connection between two people is their _____.
questions.explanation: Relationship (mối quan hệ) mô tả cách hai người liên kết.

questions.questionText: If you believe someone will not hurt you, you _____ them.
questions.explanation: Trust (tin tưởng) nghĩa là tin người đó trung thực.

exercises.title: Practice: Match the Pairs
exercises.instructions: Match each word on the left with the correct meaning on the right.

questions.questionText: Match the relationship words to their meanings.
questions.explanation: Mỗi từ khớp với một nghĩa rõ ràng. Argue và forgive thường đi cùng nhau trong mâu thuẫn.

pairs.right: to stop being angry with someone

exercises.title: Check: Vocab Quiz
exercises.instructions: No hints — choose the best answer for each question.

questions.questionText: She felt _____ when her best friend spent time with someone else.
questions.explanation: Jealous (ghen tị) khi lo bạn bè quan tâm người khác hơn.

questions.questionText: Good friends always _____ each other in difficult times.
questions.explanation: Support (hỗ trợ) nghĩa là giúp đỡ lẫn nhau.

questions.questionText: After they argued, he asked her to _____ him.
questions.explanation: Forgive (tha thứ) sau khi cãi nhau và xin lỗi.

questions.questionText: Their _____ grew stronger after they helped each other every day.
questions.explanation: Friendship (tình bạn) phát triển khi bạn hỗ trợ nhau.

questions.questionText: My dad's _____ from work came to our barbecue last Sunday.
questions.explanation: Colleague (đồng nghiệp) là người làm cùng nơi.

exercises.title: Apply: Words in Sentences
exercises.instructions: Complete each sentence with the correct word from the unit.

questions.questionText: Complete: I got an [0] to Hana's party. | Our [1] waters our plants when we travel. | Please [2] — I broke your cup.
questions.explanation: invitation (lời mời); neighbour (hàng xóm); apologise (xin lỗi).

content.template: I got an [0] to Hana's party. Our [1] waters our plants when we travel. Please [2] — I broke your cup.

questions.questionText: Complete: I [0] my sister on her new job. | We should [1] each other and not [2] about small things.
questions.explanation: congratulated (chúc mừng); trust (tin tưởng); argue (cãi nhau).

content.template: I [0] my sister on her new job. We should [1] each other and not [2] about small things.

exercises.title: Review: Vocab Quiz
exercises.instructions: No hints — choose the best answer for each question.

questions.questionText: She felt _____ when her best friend spent time with someone else.
questions.explanation: Jealous (ghen tị) khi lo bạn bè quan tâm người khác hơn.

questions.questionText: Good friends always _____ each other in difficult times.
questions.explanation: Support (hỗ trợ) nghĩa là giúp đỡ lẫn nhau.

questions.questionText: After they argued, he asked her to _____ him.
questions.explanation: Forgive (tha thứ) sau khi cãi nhau và xin lỗi.

questions.questionText: Their _____ grew stronger after they helped each other every day.
questions.explanation: Friendship (tình bạn) phát triển khi bạn hỗ trợ nhau.

questions.questionText: My dad's _____ from work came to our barbecue last Sunday.
questions.explanation: Colleague (đồng nghiệp) là người làm cùng nơi.

lessons.title: Lesson 2: Experience and Connecting Ideas
lessons.learningObjective: Use present perfect with ever/never and conjunctions because, when and although to talk about relationships.

exercises.title: Learn: Ever, Never and Conjunctions
exercises.instructions: Choose the correct word or phrase to complete each sentence.

questions.questionText: I have _____ argued with my best friend.
questions.explanation: Never + present perfect: have never argued.

questions.questionText: _____ you ever congratulated a friend online?
questions.explanation: Have you ever...? — câu hỏi kinh nghiệm.

questions.questionText: I apologised _____ I was late to the party.
questions.explanation: Because + lý do: I was late.

questions.questionText: _____ we are neighbours, we are also close friends.
questions.explanation: Although + contrast: neighbours but also friends.

questions.questionText: _____ I saw the invitation, I called my friend immediately.
questions.explanation: When + thời điểm: at the moment I saw it.

questions.questionText: She has _____ forgiven him after the argument.
questions.explanation: Present perfect: has forgiven (past participle).

exercises.title: Practice: Complete the Grammar
exercises.instructions: Fill in each gap with the correct form.

questions.questionText: Complete: I [0] never had a serious argument with my neighbour. | [1] you ever trusted a new friend quickly? | She apologised [2] she forgot the invitation.
questions.explanation: have (present perfect); Have (question); because (reason).

content.template: I [0] never had a serious argument with my neighbour. [1] you ever trusted a new friend quickly? She apologised [2] she forgot the invitation.

questions.questionText: Complete: [0] they argue, they usually forgive each other. | [1] we live on the same street, we don't know each other well. | He has [2] congratulated a colleague online.
questions.explanation: When (time); Although (contrast); never (experience).

content.template: [0] they argue, they usually forgive each other. [1] we live on the same street, we don't know each other well. He has [2] congratulated a colleague online.

exercises.title: Check: Grammar Challenge
exercises.instructions: Choose the correct answer. Think carefully.

questions.questionText: My neighbour and I have known each other _____ five years.
questions.explanation: For + khoảng thời gian với present perfect.

questions.questionText: _____ I was jealous, I didn't argue with my friend.
questions.explanation: Although + contrast: felt jealous but stayed calm.

choices.distractorNote: Would mean jealousy caused no argument

questions.questionText: Have you ever _____ to a neighbour for playing loud music?
questions.explanation: Have you ever apologised — past participle after ever.

questions.questionText: I called her _____ I wanted to congratulate her.
questions.explanation: Because gives the reason for calling.

choices.distractorNote: When focuses on time of calling

exercises.title: Apply: Build the Sentence
exercises.instructions: Put the words in the correct order to make a sentence.

questions.questionText: Make a sentence: I / have never / argued / with / my neighbour.
questions.explanation: I have never argued with my neighbour. — present perfect + never.

questions.questionText: Make a sentence: Although / we argue / sometimes, / we / always / forgive / each other.
questions.explanation: Although we argue sometimes, we always forgive each other. — although + contrast.

questions.questionText: Make a sentence: I / apologised / because / I / was / late.
questions.explanation: I apologised because I was late. — because + reason.

exercises.title: Review: Grammar Challenge
exercises.instructions: Choose the correct answer. Think carefully.

questions.questionText: My neighbour and I have known each other _____ five years.
questions.explanation: For + khoảng thời gian với present perfect.

questions.questionText: _____ I was jealous, I didn't argue with my friend.
questions.explanation: Although + contrast: felt jealous but stayed calm.

choices.distractorNote: Would mean jealousy caused no argument

questions.questionText: Have you ever _____ to a neighbour for playing loud music?
questions.explanation: Have you ever apologised — past participle after ever.

questions.questionText: I called her _____ I wanted to congratulate her.
questions.explanation: Because gives the reason for calling.

choices.distractorNote: When focuses on time of calling

lessons.title: Lesson 3: Neighbour Opinion Text
lessons.learningObjective: Read a short online post and understand opinions, details and vocabulary in context.

exercises.title: Learn: Read the Online Post
exercises.instructions: Read the post carefully. Answer the detail questions.

passage.title: Online Advice: Neighbours and Noise
passage.text:
Posted by Linh, age 13

Last week my neighbour played loud music every evening. I felt angry because I couldn't do my homework. My mum said I should talk to him politely first.

I knocked on his door when he came home from work. I explained the problem and he apologised immediately. He didn't know the music was so loud in my room.

In my opinion, good neighbours communicate before they argue. Although we are different ages, we now have a better relationship. I have never had a serious argument with a neighbour before, but this experience taught me to speak up calmly.

What do you think? Should young people always tell an adult first?
passage.imagePrompt: A teenager and an adult neighbour talking at a front door; friendly but serious conversation about noise.

questions.questionText: Why did Linh feel angry at first?
questions.explanation: Loud music stopped her doing homework.

questions.questionText: What did Linh's mum suggest?
questions.explanation: Talk to him politely first.

exercises.title: Practice: Words in the Post
exercises.instructions: Read the post again. Answer about vocabulary and meaning.

passage.title: Online Advice: Neighbours and Noise
passage.text:

In my opinion, good neighbours communicate before they argue. Although we are different ages, we now have a better relationship. I have never had a serious argument with a neighbour before, but this experience taught me to speak up calmly.

What do you think? Should young people always tell an adult first?
passage.imagePrompt: A teenager and an adult neighbour talking at a front door; friendly but serious conversation about noise.

questions.questionText: In the post, 'speak up calmly' means to _____.
questions.explanation: Nói rõ vấn đề một cách bình tĩnh, không la lớn.

choices.text: say what you think in a calm way

questions.questionText: Why does Linh think the neighbour apologised quickly?
questions.explanation: He didn't know the music was so loud in her room.

choices.text: He didn't realise the music was loud for her

exercises.title: Check: Main Idea and Order
exercises.instructions: Read the post one more time. These questions need careful thinking.

They should talk politely before arguing

questions.questionText: Put the events in order: (1) Neighbour apologises (2) Linh talks to neighbour (3) Loud music problem
questions.explanation: Problem → talk → apology.

exercises.title: Apply: Match Facts from the Post
exercises.instructions: Use what you read. Match each item to the correct fact.

lessons.title: Lesson 4: Invitations and Apologies
lessons.learningObjective: Listen to short extracts about invitations and apologies and identify main ideas and details.

exercises.title: Learn: Birthday Party Invitation
exercises.instructions: Listen to the phone call. Answer the first two questions.

script.title: Birthday Party Invitation

lines.speaker: Hana
lines.text: Hi Tom! I'm calling about my birthday party on Saturday.

lines.speaker: Tom
lines.text: Oh great! Is it at your house?

lines.speaker: Hana
lines.text: No, we're having it in the park near the statue. The invitation says four o'clock.

lines.speaker: Tom
lines.text: Can I bring my neighbour, Minh? He's new in our street.

lines.speaker: Hana
lines.text: Of course! Tell him to bring a football. We'll play before the cake.

lines.speaker: Tom
lines.text: Perfect. I'll congratulate you again on Saturday!

script.audioNotes: Friendly teenage voices, clear pace. Approx. 35 seconds.

questions.questionText: What is the call mainly about?
questions.explanation: Hana calls about her birthday party on Saturday.

questions.questionText: Where is the party?
questions.explanation: In the park near the statue.

choices.distractorNote: Tom asked but Hana said no

choices.text: In the park near the statue

exercises.title: Practice: More from the Invitation Call
exercises.instructions: Listen again to the same call. Answer the next questions.

script.title: Birthday Party Invitation

lines.speaker: Hana
lines.text: Hi Tom! I'm calling about my birthday party on Saturday.

lines.speaker: Tom
lines.text: Oh great! Is it at your house?

lines.speaker: Hana
lines.text: No, we're having it in the park near the statue. The invitation says four o'clock.

lines.speaker: Tom
lines.text: Can I bring my neighbour, Minh? He's new in our street.

lines.speaker: Hana
lines.text: Of course! Tell him to bring a football. We'll play before the cake.

lines.speaker: Tom
lines.text: Perfect. I'll congratulate you again on Saturday!

script.audioNotes: Friendly teenage voices, clear pace. Approx. 35 seconds.

questions.questionText: Who does Tom want to bring?
questions.explanation: His neighbour Minh, who is new in the street.

questions.questionText: What should Minh bring?
questions.explanation: Tell him to bring a football.

choices.distractorNote: Topic of call, not to bring
They will have cake at party

exercises.title: Check: Apologising at Work
exercises.instructions: Listen to a new conversation. Choose the correct answer.

script.title: Apologising to a Colleague

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: Mrs Pham, can I speak to you for a minute?

lines.speaker: Mrs Pham
lines.text: Yes, David. What's wrong?

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: I want to apologise because I was late to the meeting this morning.

lines.speaker: Mrs Pham
lines.text: Thank you for telling me. Why were you late?

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: My bus broke down. I have never been late before.

lines.speaker: Mrs Pham
lines.text: I understand. Please call when that happens. I trust you will try harder.

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: I will. Thank you for your support.

script.audioNotes: Calm adult voices, polite tone. Approx. 40 seconds.

questions.questionText: Why does David want to speak to Mrs Pham?
questions.explanation: He wants to apologise for being late.

choices.text: To invite her to a party

questions.questionText: Why was David late?
questions.explanation: His bus broke down.

questions.questionText: What does Mrs Pham ask David to do next time?
questions.explanation: Please call when that happens.

choices.text: Call her when he will be late

lessons.title: Lesson 5: Apologise or Accept
lessons.learningObjective: Write a short message apologising or accepting an invitation using relationship vocabulary and conjunctions.

exercises.title: Learn: Message Phrases
exercises.instructions: Complete each gap with a word from the unit.

questions.questionText: Complete: Hi Mai, | I'm sorry — I need to [0]. | I was late [1] my bus broke down. | Thank you for the [2].
questions.explanation: apologise (xin lỗi); because (lý do); invitation (lời mời).

content.template:
Hi Mai,

I'm sorry — I need to [0]. I was late [1] my bus broke down.

Thank you for the [2].

exercises.title: Practice: Build Message Sentences
exercises.instructions: Put the words in the correct order to make correct sentences for a message.

questions.questionText: Make a sentence: I / have never / missed / your / party / before.
questions.explanation: I have never missed your party before. — present perfect experience.

questions.questionText: Make a sentence: Although / I / was / busy, / I / will / come.
questions.explanation: Although I was busy, I will come. — although + contrast in a reply.

exercises.title: Check: Apology or Invitation Reply
exercises.instructions: Write a short message. Write at least 25 words.

content.taskDescription: Your friend Hana sent you an invitation to her birthday party in the park on Saturday. You were late to school today and want to apologise to your teacher in a message. Write ONE short message — choose either replying to Hana OR apologising to your teacher.
prompts: Say who you are writing to and why.
Use because, when or although in at least one sentence.
Use at least two unit words (e.g. invitation, apologise, neighbour, congratulate).
successCriteria: Uses at least two unit vocabulary words

modelAnswer.text:
Hi Hana,

Thank you for the invitation to your party on Saturday. Although I have a football match in the morning, I will come to the park at four o'clock. I have never been to a party near the statue before!

See you,
Linh

grammar.criteria: Uses present perfect with ever/never or conjunctions because/when/although correctly.

vocabulary.criteria: Uses relationship words (invitation, apologise, neighbour, trust) appropriately.

organization.criteria: Message has greeting, clear purpose and polite closing.

taskAchievement.criteria: Replies to invitation OR apologises clearly; at least 25 words.

questions.questionText: Write your response following the task.
questions.explanation: Chấm theo rubric. Bài mẫu cho thấy độ dài và nội dung mong đợi ở A2.

exercises.title: Apply: Complete the Message Frames
exercises.instructions: Complete the message frames with the correct unit words.

questions.questionText: Complete: Dear Mrs Pham, | I [0] because I was late today. | [1] the bus broke down, I couldn't call. | I hope you can [2] me.
questions.explanation: apologise; Because/When (Because fits reason); forgive.

content.template:
Dear Mrs Pham,

I [0] because I was late today. [1] the bus broke down, I couldn't call.

I hope you can [2] me.

exercises.title: Review: Apology or Invitation Reply
exercises.instructions: Write a short message. Write at least 25 words.

content.taskDescription: Your friend Hana sent you an invitation to her birthday party in the park on Saturday. You were late to school today and want to apologise to your teacher in a message. Write ONE short message — choose either replying to Hana OR apologising to your teacher.
prompts: Say who you are writing to and why.
Use because, when or although in at least one sentence.
Use at least two unit words (e.g. invitation, apologise, neighbour, congratulate).
successCriteria: Uses at least two unit vocabulary words

modelAnswer.text:
Hi Hana,

Thank you for the invitation to your party on Saturday. Although I have a football match in the morning, I will come to the park at four o'clock. I have never been to a party near the statue before!

See you,
Linh

grammar.criteria: Uses present perfect with ever/never or conjunctions because/when/although correctly.

vocabulary.criteria: Uses relationship words (invitation, apologise, neighbour, trust) appropriately.

organization.criteria: Message has greeting, clear purpose and polite closing.

taskAchievement.criteria: Replies to invitation OR apologises clearly; at least 25 words.

questions.questionText: Write your response following the task.
questions.explanation: Chấm theo rubric. Bài mẫu cho thấy độ dài và nội dung mong đợi ở A2.

lessons.title: Lesson 6: Talk About Relationships
lessons.learningObjective: Answer interview questions about friends, neighbours and relationships using unit vocabulary and grammar.

exercises.title: Learn: Choose the Best Reply
exercises.instructions: Imagine an examiner asks you a question. Choose the best answer.

questions.questionText: Examiner: Do you know your neighbours well? You say:
questions.explanation: Mô tả mối quan hệ với hàng xóm — câu trả lời tự nhiên A2.

choices.text: Yes, I invitation them every day.
Yes, we often chat when we see each other.
Yes, I argue my neighbour is a door.

questions.questionText: Examiner: Have you ever congratulated a friend online? You say:
questions.explanation: Present perfect + ever: Yes, I have...

choices.text: Yes, I never congratulate friends online.
Yes, I have congratulated friends on social media.

questions.questionText: Examiner: What do you do when you argue with a friend? You say:
questions.explanation: Forgive + apologise — giải quyết mâu thuẫn.

choices.text: We usually invitation and trust each other.
We usually apologise and forgive each other.
We usually jealous and colleague each other.

exercises.title: Practice: Best Response
exercises.instructions: Choose the best phrase you would say in each situation.

questions.questionText: Examiner: Why is trust important in a friendship? You say:
questions.explanation: Because + lý do — trust helps you share secrets.

choices.text: Because you can argue every day.
choices.distractorNote: Negative, not a reason for trust

choices.text: Because you can tell your friend private things.
Because you need an invitation to school.

questions.questionText: Examiner: When do you usually see your colleagues? You say:
questions.explanation: When + thời gian: at work on weekdays.

choices.text: Although I see colleagues at the park statue.
When I help my mum at her shop on Saturdays.
Because I congratulate colleagues at midnight.

questions.questionText: Examiner: Tell me about a good friendship. You say:
questions.explanation: Support + friendship — mô tả tình bạn tích cực.

choices.text: We jealous each other although we trust.
We support each other although we sometimes argue.
We neighbour each other when we forgive.

exercises.title: Check: Relationships Interview
exercises.instructions: Answer the examiner's questions about friends and neighbours. Speak for up to two minutes.

content.prompt: The examiner will ask you about your friends, neighbours and how you solve problems in relationships.
content.pictureDescription: Two teenagers talking in a park; one holds a party invitation, the other waves to a neighbour across the street.
followUpQuestions: Do you know your neighbours well?
Have you ever argued with a friend?
How do you usually congratulate your friends?
Who do you trust with your secrets?
When do you apologise to someone?
Although friends sometimes disagree, how do you stay friends?
suggestedAnswers: Yes, our neighbour often helps us and we trust her.
Yes, I have argued with my cousin about games, but we forgive each other.
I congratulate them in person or send a message online.
I trust my best friend because she never tells my secrets.
I apologise when I am late or say something unkind.
We support each other and talk calmly because friendship is important.

assessmentCriteria.pronunciation: Key words (neighbour, invitation, apologise, relationship, trust) are understandable.
assessmentCriteria.fluency: Responds with phrases or short sentences without long silences.
assessmentCriteria.grammar: Uses present perfect with ever/never or conjunctions because/when/although in at least two answers.
assessmentCriteria.vocabulary: Uses at least four different unit words correctly.

questions.questionText: Record your answers to the follow-up questions.
questions.explanation: Chấm tổng thể theo bốn tiêu chí. Trả lời ngắn vẫn được điểm một phần nếu dùng đúng từ vựng.

exercises.title: Apply: Situational Responses
exercises.instructions: Choose the best thing to say in each situation.

questions.questionText: Your friend is jealous because you went to a party without them. You say:
questions.explanation: Apologise + invitation for next time — xử lý tình huống nhạy cảm.

choices.text: I'm sorry. I will argue you next time because I am jealous.
I'm sorry. I will invite you next time because our friendship is important.
I'm sorry. I will colleague you next time at the statue.

questions.questionText: A new neighbour moves in. You want to be friendly. You say:
questions.explanation: Chào hàng xóm mới — câu đơn giản lịch sự.

choices.text: Hello! Welcome to the forgive. I'm your invitation next door.
Hello! Welcome to the street. I'm your neighbour next door.
Hello! Welcome to the argue. I'm your trust next door.

questions.questionText: The examiner asks: Have you ever had to forgive a friend? You say:
questions.explanation: Present perfect experience + forgive.

choices.text: Yes, I have forgive a friend although we invitation.
Yes, I have forgiven a friend after we argued about a game.
Yes, I never have forgiven although I always argue happily.

Yes, I have argued with my cousin about games, but we forgive each other.
I congratulate them in person or send a message online.
I trust my best friend because she never tells my secrets.
I apologise when I am late or say something unkind.
We support each other and talk calmly because friendship is important.
